summary: Screened-Poisson reconstruction with explicit lambda data-fidelity weight: inside the mask solve (Δ - lambda·I)u = Δguidance - lambda·anchor with a Dirichlet anchor boundary, per channel by row-major Gauss-Seidel. lambda = 0 is pure Poisson (guidance gradients); large lambda approaches the anchor. Deterministic (bit-identical reruns); the report carries SolverData.

inputs.name: guidance
inputs.doc: The image whose gradients drive the masked interior (the guidance divergence Δguidance).

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.doc: The data-fidelity target: the Dirichlet boundary and the field the interior is pulled toward with weight lambda.

inputs.name: mask
inputs.doc: The coverage mask selecting the interior region to reconstruct (> 0.5 is interior).

outputs.name: candidate
outputs.doc: The reconstructed image (the anchor descriptor; same extent/layout).

outputs.name: report
outputs.doc: The solver report carrying SolverData (iterations, residual history, stop reason, tolerance, final residual).

params.name: lambda
params.doc: The data-fidelity weight (>= 0). 0 is pure Poisson (guidance gradients); a large value pulls the interior toward the anchor.

params.name: max_iterations
params.doc: The maximum Gauss-Seidel sweeps before the solver stops at the cap, in 1..=100000.

perceptual.reason: repair.screened_poisson is a screened-Poisson solver verified by analytic lambda-limit fixtures (lambda = 0 recovers pure Poisson, large lambda snaps to the anchor), boundary continuity, bit-identical reruns, and a residual-history convergence record; there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
